statements.explanation: She became Queen on 6th February, 1952. The 2nd of June, 1953 was her coronation day.
The text says 'Kings and queens and other important people came to London from all over the world to be at the ceremony.'

statements.statement: Other kings and queens were at the ceremony.
statements.explanation: The text explicitly states 'Kings and queens and other important people came to London...'

statements.statement: The Queen rode through London in a white coach.
statements.explanation: She rode in a gold coach. The text says '...drove through London in a gold coach pulled by white horses.'
The text says 'Hundreds of soldiers in different uniforms rode past wearing shining swords.'

statements.statement: People had waited a long time to see the Queen.
statements.explanation: The text mentions 'Some people had waited in the streets for days to get a good place to watch.'
The text says '...it was raining in London and everybody got wet. Except the Queen. Her coach had a roof.'

statements.statement: The speaker was at the ceremony in London.
statements.explanation: The speaker says 'I watched the ceremony on television' and 'My family didn't live in London.'
The text says 'My family didn't live in London...'
The speaker says 'Every child, including me... was given a present.'

statements.statement: There was a picture of the King on the present.
statements.explanation: The present had a picture of the new Queen on it.

statements.statement: The speaker liked the music best.
statements.explanation: The text cuts off, but the teacher guide implies she liked the fireworks best.
